what_it_does: Defines a static HTML presentation piece showing two mobile app screens: an all-pieces ledger and a piece-detail drill-in, with rows and icon rails populated by inline JavaScript.
observations: Imports Plus Jakarta Sans and IBM Plex Mono from Google Fonts plus shared piece and phone stylesheets.
Uses two phone mockups inside a titled composition with captions describing ledger and drill-in roles.
The first phone shows category pills, a generated ledger list of eight pieces, a compose action, and a bottom icon rail.
The second phone shows detail metrics, two content plates for dials and notes, compare/open actions, and a matching bottom icon rail.
Inline script builds rows from a local array and icons from the global DNA.org.icon helper.
standouts: Ledger and detail views are shown side by side as related app states.
The row list encodes piece titles with short type labels such as stack, bands, field, grid, duo, flow, and tiers.
Warm palette variables and repeated phone primitives tie the two screens into one visual system.
conventions: Follows a static piece pattern with shared surface CSS, phone component classes, and a small inline script for generated markup.
Uses CSS custom properties for spacing, color, and theme tokens.
Uses IDs as insertion points for generated rows, rail icons, and plate icons.
Mixes declarative HTML structure with local JavaScript data arrays for repeated UI elements.
concerns: The file depends on external shared styles and the DNA.org runtime being present.
Inline style attributes are used for per-element color and spacing choices.
Rows are generated with innerHTML from local string data, which is acceptable here but keeps markup and data tightly coupled.
notes: This file is a mobile app ledger/detail mockup for browsing pieces and opening a selected piece.
questions: What interaction, if any, is expected from the category pills, rows, compare action, and bottom rail?
What does the mark "V" signify within this piece?
Are the row type labels intended as taxonomy, layout descriptors, or visual metaphors?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated ledger-screen purpose matches the file: it presents an all-pieces ledger and a related drill-in detail screen.
apparent_themes: structured drill-in from collection to detail
intention_signals: It seems intended to express a composed app experience where many design/code pieces can be browsed as a ledger, filtered by broad groups, and inspected through detail metrics and notes.
novelty: The file treats a creative-piece archive as a tactile mobile ledger with taxonomy pills, generated artifact rows, and a paired detail state in the same composition.
contribution: Contributes a ledger-and-detail mobile pattern for holding many pieces, including category grouping, artifact rows with type labels, piece metadata bands, action hierarchy, and shared navigation rail treatment.
summary_for_embedding: Static HTML piece rendering two mobile app mockups for an all-pieces ledger and a selected piece detail view, using shared surface styles and DNA icon helpers.
intention_for_embedding: This was for visualizing a calm mobile ledger interface that organizes creative/code/design pieces and supports drilling into an individual piece.
